hours_formula_detail: KEESM 2520 requires 'working at least 20 hours per week (averaged monthly)' = 80 hours/month under 7 CFR 273.24. Kansas uses NO workfare allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage formula; the community-service/volunteer lane satisfies the same flat 80 hrs/month standard. In-kind work and unpaid employment (volunteering) explicitly count.
minimum_wage_note: N/A for federal_80 classification (no allotment/min-wage formula). For reference, Kansas state minimum wage is $7.25/hr (equal to the federal floor), but it is not used in any ABAWD hours calculation.
qualifying_org_definition: A nonprofit organization (501(c)(3) or State of Kansas not-for-profit). KEESM 2520 and DCF guidance state ABAWD work may be 'for pay, for goods or services, unpaid, or as a volunteer'; in-kind/unpaid volunteering at a nonprofit explicitly counts.
verification_instrument: Signed letter or statement from a supervisor or nonprofit organization documenting volunteer hours (per DCF guidance cited in KEESM 1322.1(8)); timesheets also accepted. No named Kansas-specific form number exists for the general volunteer lane. KEESM 2520 states 'Verification that the individual is working an average of 20 hours per week is mandatory.'
required_prestep_detail: No EDP, county assignment, MOU, or approved-site registration is required for the general volunteer lane under KEESM 2520. The E-26 work registration is application-intake paperwork completed when first enrolling in SNAP, not a gate on volunteer hours counting. The formal E&T worksite track (KEESM 3310) requiring ES-4104/ES-4105 cooperative agreements is a SEPARATE pathway and does not bind the volunteer lane.
accepts_outofstate_detail: KEESM 2520, KEESM 1322, and DCF guidance cite 'a nonprofit organization' / '501(c)(3)' without any in-state location requirement. No Kansas policy restricts the certifying organization to a Kansas-based entity; the federal default (7 CFR 273.24) imposes no such restriction. Operationally an out-of-state 501(c)(3) signing verification letters should be accepted, but this is inferred, not affirmatively stated.
geographic_waivers: None. Kansas holds no statewide or county-level ABAWD waivers. Under OBBBA/HR1 (2025) waivers are limited to areas with unemployment >10%, and FNS terminated outdated-criteria waivers on Nov 3, 2025. Kansas had no prior waivers, so OBBBA did not change Kansas waiver status. All 105 counties subject to the ABAWD work requirement.
submission_deadline: Kansas uses a change-reporting model: recipients must report when work hours drop below 20/week soon after the change becomes known. Positive verification of qualifying hours is typically presented at recertification or on caseworker request, not on a fixed monthly schedule. No specific day-of-month deadline found.
obbba_fy2026_impact: OBBBA/HR1 (signed July 4, 2025; full implementation Nov 1, 2025) raised the ABAWD upper age limit to 64 (from 54), reinstated the requirement for veterans, former foster youth, and people experiencing homelessness, and added an 80-hrs/month requirement for adults in households with children age 14+. These changes EXPAND the population subject to the requirement in Kansas but do NOT alter the volunteer/community-service pathway mechanism: 80 hrs/month of qualifying activity (including unpaid nonprofit volunteering) still satisfies the work requirement, with no workfare formula introduced.
notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-22 for FY2026 (Oct 2025-Sep 2026) under OBBBA/HR1. KEESM 2520 (fetched directly) confirms verbatim: 'Working at least 20 hours per week (averaged monthly)', 'This includes in-kind work and unpaid employment (volunteering)', and 'Verification that the individual is working an average of 20 hours per week is mandatory.' This is the flat federal 80-hrs/month standard (federal_80) — Kansas uses NO allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage workfare formula, so minimum_wage_usd is set to 0 per schema. The general volunteer lane is clean: any qualifying nonprofit, no EDP/county assignment/MOU/approved-site list, no prestep beyond ordinary SNAP application. Verification is a signed nonprofit letter or timesheet; no named Kansas form exists (verification_form_name = unknown). Out-of-state nonprofit and remote stance are SILENT in Kansas policy (no restriction found, acceptance inferred but not stated). OBBBA expanded the affected population (age ceiling 64, fewer exemptions, household-with-teen rule) and FNS terminated old-criteria waivers Nov 3, 2025; Kansas had no waivers and remains fully enforced across all 105 counties. Confidence HIGH on classification + hours + enforcement; the only residual uncertainty is the absence of a named verification form and the inferred (not stated) acceptance of out-of-state/remote volunteering.
unresolved: No named Kansas verification form for the volunteer lane (signed nonprofit letter/timesheet is the de facto instrument).
Out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance is inferred from absence of restriction, not affirmatively stated in policy.
Remote/virtual volunteering acceptance is silent in Kansas policy.
FY26 E&T State Plan PDF is binary/non-parseable; corroborated via metadata and other sources rather than direct text extraction.
